news.title: How AI guardrails are impeding the work of offensive cybersecurity researchers
news.summary: We spoke with several cybersecurity researchers, who look for unknown vulnerabilities and develop tools to exploit them, about how OpenAI’s and Anthropic’s guardrails affect their work.

news.title: Meet the judges who will crown Australia’s next breakout startup
news.summary: TechCrunch Startup Battlefield is coming to Australia — and we're partnering with Stripe to find the country's most exciting early-stage startups.

news.summary: AMD is challenging its chipmaker rival with a new rack-scale system that will start shipping to customers later this year.

news.title: Patreon lays off 20% of its workforce
news.summary: In a memo to staff that was posted online, Conte said the company's core business is strong, but the platform has to respond to market changes and adjust its cost structure to remain stable.

news.title: Alexa Plus is getting an AI update to handle more complicated instructions
news.summary: Amazon is launching an update to its Alexa Plus assistant that will allow it to connect to smart home devices in new ways. news.summary: Split between buying a smart calendar, a kitchen TV, a smart home hub, and a smart display? Amazon’s Echo Show 21 is all of those things in one, with a huge 21-inch screen. news.summary: Until now, voice mode has only been available on Claude Haiku, Anthropic's faster but less powerful model. news.title: Patreon is laying off 20 percent of workers
news.summary: Patreon is laying off 20 percent of its workers, or around 93 employees, as reported earlier by 404 Media. news.summary: Google's selfie videos can be used for account access, AI Avatars, and age verification.

news.title: An FDA Panel Just Endorsed These Unproven Peptides

news.title: For Taylor Swift, Madison Square Garden’s Controversial Cameras Briefly Went Dark
news.summary: MSG’s sprawling surveillance system can monitor guests down to the second. Its owners made an exception for the pop star’s rehearsal dinner.

news.title: The EU Fines Google $1 Billion for Prioritizing Its Own Services in Search
news.summary: The European Commission claims that Google boosted its own apps and products to the top of search rankings to the detriment of its competitors.
